source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It began to dawn on me that fear of future suffering is a major issue of aging and it is not consciously dealt with by people.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And our general tendency is to respond to those issues with massive denial, massive denial.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And what most people do when that stuff we've done on time, we just did it the chapter on time, is as the future gets shorter.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And it gets filled more with things that are shadowy, that are dark to deal with.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: There's a tendency of the mind to either just tighten up and control everything to try to push against, to protect from, or also to go back into the past and live in reverie and sentimentality and old stories and things like that.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And we said wouldn't it be nice if people could come into the present as a process?

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But the predicament is you can't fully be in the present if you have massive denial going.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: In other words, the future is corrupting the present because of the way you deal with it, which is very interesting.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And if you want to be in the present you have to fold the future into the present. You have to bring it into this consciousness.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So if there is in the future a good probability of experiencing those things which generally lead to suffering and that probability gets greater the older you get.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And even if you know that you're a very spiritual person and that suffering is great.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Even if you know that you're still going to do your best to avoid the suffering because you're not a massacist.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: You're just a spiritually evolved being.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So you work to prevent a new work through maintaining your health through a lot of things you do to keep your game projected into the future as a healthy and long and happy one.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But to the extent that you do it as affirmations is if you're never going to get this shadows, you're on thin ice.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And thin ice creates fear. There's fear when you walk it on thin ice. So it's interesting to see whether or not the preparation for future suffering isn't to deal with the issue of future suffering rather than pushing it under the rug and making believe it will go away or thinking just positively or whatever.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I mean at least be friend of the thing you're frightened of is sort of a strategy. You're going to bring it into the present moment.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It might still be frightening, but it's also something that you will see as just a part of process of the universe.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: In other words, coming to terms with future fears is bringing them into a slightly different perspective than the one in which the fear is generated.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: There are a lot of things in the future in terms of physical breakdown and mental breakdown and then dying and then death.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And then some people are even afraid of what happens after death.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So we've got quite a list of things here to have in our pool of things that generate fear that lead to denial.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So most people treat themselves as if they're immortal in the sense that they aren't going to die and nothing's going to happen.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Feeling that that's the best strategy to enjoy the moment.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The predicament is that if there is denial and if there is fear in the situation, you're not fully in the moment.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Some of your energy is what Freud just called a effective energy. It's used to keep something out of your consciousness.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So when you realize this, what I'm talking about, when you realize that, then you say, well, if I would like to be free to be fully in the present moment, then in some way I have to have commerce with this suffering, this fear of suffering, this future suffering, this future stuff of death and dying and all.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I have to in some way, I don't exactly want to say neutralize it, but in some way, defang it or make it habitable as a space.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: If it's only the due change so that you are now somebody who can compare what you previously thought was unbearable.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Basically, so if you say that, then you say, well, how would I do that? How would I, how will I have commerce with future suffering?

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I think that the reason that I got into working with dying people was because for two reasons, one, as I pointed out in previous time, was that because of the experiences I had had, I had had a different attitude towards death than the people around me, and therefore there was some value in bringing that attitude to the presence of the dying person.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But from my point of view, what I experienced was that the kind of attachments that human beings have, that keep them caught in what Buddha calls the basic ignorance, which is the illusion of the reality of your own separateness,

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: or your own existence is a separate entity exclusively as if you are real.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So what I wanted to do was to deal with my attachments, my versions and my attractions, which were the things that my mind would cling to, which would keep me solid.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I wanted to get free. And what I saw was that in human in the species, I guess in most living species, the root attachment is to survival as a separate entity.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And I mean, I could deal, I started to work with my sexual hongers and fears and all of stuff, and I started to work with my power and issues of power and fame and all of stuff.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And I started to deeply open to compassion. I'm describing the shockers now.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: See, but the first shocker, which is really the survival one, was one that is so deep in you. It's that last gas for breath when you're drowning, or it's the whatever that clawing it to keep separate.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And it's continued to exist.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: That I saw that the closest way I could get to that was hanging out with people who were dying other than to die.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And more I hung out with them, and the more I could open myself to the fullness of what that was, the more I could die before I die.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So I need not die when I die. Is the what the spiritual phrasing is of that? Die in the morning. So you need not die at night. The other way of saying that same thing. Same thing.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So one of the ways that I have found, and it is succeeded beyond my wildest dreams, of bringing the future into the present, was starting to hang out with beings who were the future.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Who were dying or aging or chronically ill or decaying or whatever. And how interesting it has been. I in terms of not only the beauty of our relationships and the love we've experienced with one of them, but the incredible growth for me.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Because it's as if with each new item on the list of woes, I have to open to it freshly again.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Like I mentioned, the fellow I was working with that had ALS. And this was just a couple of months back, I guess, I wonder that was that.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And all he could do was, uh, per se slips for dots and raises eyebrows for dashes. Yeah, no more movement.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And when I came to visit him, the first experience as I sat down to just be with him was my mind was captured by my claustrophobia, by my sense of being trapped in a body.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Where you couldn't speak, you couldn't do anything, you couldn't do anything anymore. And you were still in it. So it took me a while to kind of quiet down enough, not to deny that.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But to find some of the bar part of my being, in other words, it says, it's hard to hear the subtle inner voice when the trumpets are blurring too loud.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Because it's a very quiet little inner voice. And there was really no way initially that I could hear an inner voice.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And that has happened time and time again in each situation. When I was with Kelly Niles, the fellow that we've talked about the quadroplagion, it took me almost six months to be able to meet him without being reactive to his symbolic identity.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So that I could meet him, I could be with him. And in that sense, I had eaten and digested that particular thing. It was on my plate of calm stuff to work with.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And they keep generalizing across scenes so that if you've dealt with one kind of thing, you deal with a class of events, you don't have to deal with each one.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But one of the big ones, of course, is the loss of the mind. And that intrigues me a great deal because it may well be that we are really misreading it largely because of our own, we're outstanding. Let's put it that way.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And I really saw, all right, I started to theorize. I remember it was almost a heretical thought when I thought it. Thinking, do you think the person that's getting Alzheimer's is winning instead of losing?

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And that was a no-no in the culture in which the linear analytic mind is valued.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But I started entertain that thought. And it's interesting when you've been working with planes of consciousness and moving yourself through planes of consciousness as a, as a spiritual practice and an art form as well, and a way to enrich the moment of life and see the nature of relative realities and all the stuff that I've been doing for years as everybody else that I know.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The idea that a person's mind or awareness moves to these other planes and isn't here at the same moment or is here sporadically or here unpredictably or whatever.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: May well be part of the perfection of a stage of the process of moving to the next part of one's role in the universe, whatever that may be.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And I've just been through about a month now or about a couple of months, I guess. So very interesting dialogue and interplay with Timothy Leary.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And because when Timothy heard he had a prostate cancer and it didn't even have to have a test to size so widely, he called me and he sounded like somebody about to go in a big adventure very excited.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Like you were just going to go to Fiji or you were going to somewhere exciting or you're going to do some rascally thing which Timothy loves doing. And he called me to tell me how excited he was he was going to die. And I said that's wonderful.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Because I mean Timothy has always defined the myths that we play in the mythic fields we play in so he was just doing it again. So he's going to myth is any wonderful.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So that started a relationship and the relationship because we hadn't spent, we saw each other once a twice a year but and it was fun but we had no business together really.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: For an interesting discussion we can have some other time but now we had new business because he was dying and he was a dear friend and if I could be of any service to him and also I'll kind of wanted a hang around to see how he did it.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I mean if you've got somebody that you've been traveling within consciousness for years and years then you want to watch and see what they do with it.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So when I got involved with Timothy the first thing I noticed when I started to get down to the LA to be with him.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The first thing I noticed was that he had changed in that he was much more affectionate than he had been before. He was cuddly affectionate, which that was not Timothy strategy. Timothy was very very gracious and very friendly and very warm and very delighted.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: He was living in a party all the time. So long 24 hour days.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And it's a certain kind of cocktail party mentality and it just goes on and on and on and on and on and on and on and it's almost a hell realm in some ways but it's very exciting and things are happening and deals are being in it's new ideas are being promulgated and political things are changing and all of this.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It's a fun kind of juice and a adrenaline rush.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But Timothy I was a medium in a different level softer place, more fun, sweeter.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The other thing was that I walked into his house and I had walked into a cyber world and all of the people around here were young, cyber people, cyber persons.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And they were busy downloading Timothy on to his web page, which makes perfect sense if you are a cyber person because for cyber person what is relevant is information.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And therefore what we are to each other is information. Not even knowledge, just information. I mean it's really.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It's just lots of stuff. So I walked into this house which was walking into a metaphor of death. He was just downloading.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I mean, this wasn't a dying scene. He was downloading and in the process of downloading he was having a great party.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And he had a continuing cell on. He people dropping around and talking about his death and how he was going to do it and what it was about.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And the party started around noon each day and went to the morning and Tim in his electric wheelchair would float around the house.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Like a cocktail party saying hello and all talking to here and then go back and rest well.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Have nitrous oxide balloons and all of the pain medication he could possibly wish for it as wild as dreams.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And what I saw he had done was he had turned his dying as he did with everything else into a myth. He had just he was living the mythological component of it as he's done as an everything.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: You know as an escape artist as a criminal as a you know philosopher as a.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So this was a theater of dying. So I started to have a little concern as I would because I have this kind of Jewish neurotic.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Waiting for the worst all the time, you know, it's all fun but it's going to turn to shit.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So I had this feeling Timothy must be in denial because he definitely was living in some conceptual structure of what he thought the game was.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And when I said to Timothy, where's your soul he prayed it was brain.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And of course what he had planned was that besides downloading he was planning to freeze his brain in like liquid nitrogen at the moment of death.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So it all made perfect sense within a totally psychotic model of reality from my point of view.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But we always look psychotic to each other. That's the predicament and I could as well be psychotic as he which he kept pointing out.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So I mean over the years not not now he didn't but we were very subtle now about dealing with our differences.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: About whether there is anything after death or what is after death.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And we agreed that neither of us knew that that was the mystery and that the Tibetans had a model and the Indians had a model and the Chinese had everybody had a model.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And these were models because whatever it was it was non conceptual and our concepts weren't going to embrace it whatever it was.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So it had to do we understood that and so I thought he would might be denying so the next time I went down which is I don't know a couple of weeks later when he was getting weaker.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And we spent time good quality time together talking and then at one point we just looked into one of those eyes and we just kept looking.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And it was a quality of meeting Timothy behind his theater performance.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: This was no longer the actor on the stage. This was somebody just like me who was just here.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And we met in our hereness and immediately all my concerns about denial dissolved because he was in the same predicament I was.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I mean you don't know that doesn't mean it's denial you're just sitting with whatever it is as it comes along and he was behind it.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But what he was projecting out was this theater piece of dying which everybody was buying into as what Timothy was but it wasn't it wasn't it.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I mean I knew I knew him behind all this but I couldn't find him before because I kept getting sucked in.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So once I met that place in Timothy I felt considerable belief.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And then heard as it went on and he's got close to dying what was fun for me was two things.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: One was when I'd hang around him.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: At one point we were he was signing a.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Blotter acid art and he was being paid ten dollars a signature he was dying he's dying you know within two days he was dead and he was earning his living and I said he don't really.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And I was taking the pieces away as he signed that I said to nothing we're doing piece work.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: You know it was literally great.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But his mind was because he and I have traveled in so many realms of consciousness together.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: When he moves through realms of consciousness I'm trained to go along with him.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: There's nothing in me that's sitting where I started from saying that sounds crazy to me you know I mean that isn't who I have been that's.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And so Timothy was being very very non-sequatorish non-linear non whatever you know.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And it was delighting me because it was taking my mind and just pulling it there and there and there instead of trying to relate.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Allow the non-linearity to happen and get the gishdald and the whole and he still had his pocket humor at one point he said to me.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Yeah I think I'm losing my memory.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Richard and he said you know then memories nearly everything there is said yeah.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But you know there's something else besides memory all memories.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I said yeah but I forgot and a little smile at the corner of his mouth.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The little smile at the corner of his mouth and people were standing around us taking pictures all the time.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I mean it was continuous photo opportunities and it was interesting.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The Timothy all the pictures showing with his mouth closed and that was because his teeth were starting to from the medicines and all he started to loosen teeth.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And his image of himself was in jeopardy.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: He could smile with those bros of teeth and I remembered my mother dying when her teeth didn't work and she had a fan.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I remember I talked about her having a fan in front of her face even with me.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: She wouldn't show me what she looked like without teeth and there was Timothy on the other end of the spectrum doing exactly the same thing.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I loved it. I just love it. I mean to me I just you know.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I said to him what are you kidding Timothy? What are you telling me that?

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I said you know my herodg only had three teeth and I could never stop looking at him.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: He was so beautiful.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I like okay Timothy get on with it.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But then I heard that he had he had said to the one of the women that he was there with.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Help me get out of this or something that is in the phrase it was some phrase.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And she said I can't or something like that.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And he turned and pushed her away and he grabbed the bureau which he then hold held onto for about six or eight hours.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Very tightly and tightly.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And the description of the different stages of that and then he went into a very deep peaceful state and then he said things like

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: He came out at one point and he said publish publish publish publish.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: He cried twice.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And then he said to me at one point I think the last thing he said was why not or something like that.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Sure it was profound.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: At one point I said to him Timothy you've gone through a lot here you know you've been dying you've been exploring death.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I'm in the dying business.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: What have you found out you know tell me something interesting what's it like what's the whole process like.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I said if you learned anything he said yes I have.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Wow this is great I mean this is I said well tell me so I can share it with people.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: He said well the first thing I've learned and I lean forward.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: He said is that I'm a good boy and I said yep he said I'm a very good boy.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: He said yeah we are.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And to me the sweetness of all of the planes of consciousness playing out at one moment he's this young Catholic boy who's you know who's always a bad boy.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And the other joy I had was in having the people who are around him so available that at one what they call me each day and we get on the conference speaker phone.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And we'd all talk about what everybody was feeling and what was going on around him and we talked about how one could surround a person like when he was more in his kind of non-responsive states.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: They would hang out in the room and talk about the beautiful parts of his life which is a Tibetan tradition that you recount the the compassionate acts of a person's life at the time of dying to have them see their own incarnation in a certain way is that free to stand back and just see it.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And just to be and for them to be able to handle his non-linearity joyfully and to keep the feeling of just spaciousness spaciousness spaciousness spaciousness to life spacious allowance not ha ha ha but just and they did that and it was quite beautiful and they're very pleased and.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: That guy is in Timothy where he anybody now he would be pleased.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: What it showed me or what I learned from that process I saw how a couple of things first I saw how we had moved and I think I've been part of that movement with Stephen Levine and Elizabeth Kuba Ross and Andre and people like that.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And shanti project we have showed to the culture the value of bringing death out of the medical model which is reflected as the high temple as the ICU ward.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And superior which says keep the body alive and that's as simple as that that's what it is and death is the flat brain wave and that's it.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And the hospice movement, um, Sessley Sonders movement has done a remarkable job of becoming a mainstream cultural institution.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It did that because of two things one is that it resonated with the humanity and people.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The second was that it was economically more viable than the medical model and the third thing was that it cleaned up its act and got rid of the spiritual references so that it became a psychological support system basically physical and psychological support which.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And then what happened was since most of the people in the hospice work were farther out than the hospice institution itself.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: What you had were basically spiritual environments but under the rubric for Medicare and all of being non religious or non something that would be scary to the culture.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And then I think that I've had in attempts to create things like with Dale Borglum the living dying project and you know, ashrams that are dying places and I've talked about all that with you.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But it's been my intention to create a spaciousness that was not so busy with physical death or psychological death but could start to deal with the spiritual issues of awareness and expanded awareness at the time of dying.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: What Timothy added was the fun of it the adventure of as you approach the mystery when you're approaching a mystery what what space do you want to be in in your head.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Getting free of guilt. I mean is that the one you want which one do you want which one is like which opens to the possibilities and Timothy was an adventure.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I mean as I've contend to from being in the backwash for years. Timothy took me screaming and fighting and screaming into adventure.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And he opened up the whole image of dying into the possibility that it was a celebratory adventurous exciting part of life.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And I will I think I would like to push the Larry Method.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Train people in the Larry Method of helping people to die on a mixed cocktail.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Various swords.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: When I look at the fear of future suffering and I start a picket apart like take the one with ALS.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I go there I have claustrophobia. I feel oh my god. I'm strangled and then I slowly slowly relax relax relax come back into my center open be with him until finally that is part of the universe at the moment and here we still are.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And I realize that I wouldn't ask for that and I certainly hope I don't have to go through it, but we're I to go through it. I could go through it because I am in it.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It's it's the level of empathy you allow of how much you love and die into the beloved again and again in these different forms of it.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Because these are all the faces of the beloved that are coming at you and some of them are ones that are awake and so much a version in you you just close down and you go into some you know reactive response symbolic reactive response.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: One of the fears that people have of the future when they're getting older is that they'll be faced with predicaments that they either will be have to make a decision for decision about ending life.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: There's or somebody else's decisions about how much treatment to have and which kinds and all of that that there'll be faced with life significant decisions or whether to give up their home or

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: whatever from an economic point of view or things like that or decisions of whether to live alone or move into a community holds.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I mean there are a lot of big decisions that come along with aging and there's also the fear that you will be frail and less capable of making those decisions.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Laura Huxley I was with her last week and she's 86 and she said it is so difficult for me to make decisions now.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And it's interesting and she's been a very decisive person in her past.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But she was just opening to other planes of consciousness where decision making wasn't she didn't really have that one.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So there is a lot of living wills and all of the stuff of making plans as best you can for the time when you want to be making plans.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Which I think is a very intelligent strategy.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But I realize and hang out with Timothy how much I treasure the non-linearity of his mind and how much I would like people around people with Alzheimer's to treasure the non-linearity of mind.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: To treasure the deeper wisdom part of human beings rather than the historical knowledge carrying social identity part of the person.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And how good it would be for those caretakers in terms of their own growth to keep practicing letting go of their own linearity.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Because what will be most frightening at the time of death is your own linearity.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Is the way you're clinging to your own sense of your own identity isn't that true?

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And that's what's going to do. That's going to dissolve at the moment death.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: To the extent that you're a conceptual model and you haven't connected with the part of you that just is that isn't anything just I am not I am this or I am that is going to be trauma at the moment of dying because who you think you are isn't.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And then I mean Timothy had that adventurous spirit so that he could get scared and get frightened.

source: Ram Dass_ Approaching the Mystery _ Here & Now Podcast Ep. 276__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And at the same moment he could stay open and then keep expanding expanding expanding.
